config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse by the majority or by the state. A healthy society protects individual rights precisely because people need freedom, dignity, and limits on power. From my perspective, the better principle is that society's interests should be balanced with individual rights, not placed above them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik dan hukum di sini menunjukkan bahwa begitu negara atau mayoritas diberi prioritas mutlak, hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil cepat sekali jadi korban dengan alasan “demi kebaikan bersama.” Tentu ada situasi darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan pembenaran untuk mengorbankan individu secara permanen. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu menyeimbangkannya dengan kepentingan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how “for the public good” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, corruption, and silencing weaker voices. At the same time, I do not support an extreme individualism that ignores communal responsibilities, because Indonesian life also depends on mutual obligation and social harmony. The right balance is not to rank society above the person, but to protect individual rights while allowing limited, accountable restrictions when there is a real public need. If that balance is lost, the so-called interests of society usually end up serving those with power, not ordinary people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya di Amerika, memberi prioritas umum pada “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan atas individu. Poin tentang kebutuhan bersama memang valid, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan hak harus benar-benar sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan kabur untuk menekan minoritas atau suara yang tidak populer. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu sambil hanya membatasi seperlunya ketika ada ancaman nyata. Kalau urutannya dibalik, yang sering menang bukan “masyarakat” melainkan orang-orang yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should be put above the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, appeals to “the public interest” are often used too loosely, and that can hide abuse, favoritism, or pressure on weaker people who have little power to resist. At the same time, I do not believe in a selfish individualism; people do have obligations to family, neighbors, and the wider community, but those duties should not erase basic rights. A fair society is one that protects individual dignity first and only limits it in narrow, accountable ways when there is a real and proven common need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah politik di sini menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan yang terlalu luas, hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil sering jadi korban. Poin bahwa ada kebutuhan bersama memang benar, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan harus ketat, jelas, sementara, dan bisa diawasi, bukan memberi cek kosong kepada negara atau mayoritas. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang menekan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu sambil membatasi secara seperlunya hanya saat ada ancaman nyata.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
